summary: On 3.4.3 Opening Main Workplace, does not show up on File Manager, Links are unrecognized

description.text:
### Description

When I try to open a workplace it does not show up on the file manager. If I have a file link in the document, Zettlr does not recognize file until I control-click on it. Then it options me to overwrite file. 

However, unannoyingly, workplaces that I have NOT been working on recently do appear. 

### Reproducing

Click on open workplace, choose any folder and it will not show up on the file manager. 

### Zettlr version

3.4.3

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 10

### Additional Information

I checked the error logs in Dev tools and nothing appear wrong. Workspaces NOT used recently do open and Zettlr declares it is watching those. 

Does it matter that the folder is in my iCloud drive? 

I removed all files that were not markdown without a change. 

I place the same folder into my hard drive (from my iCloud) and it worked

summary: feat: Add transform to strip duplicate spaces

description.text:
Closes #5679

This adds one of the text transforms -- strip duplicate spaces -- from issue #5659.

I'm using this PR to both flush out the approach and learn any things about contributing to this codebase. If all goes well I'll implement the other text transforms secure in the knowledge that the approach has been signed-off by Hendrik (and others) and any "dumb" first-time contributor stuff is sorted.

## Description

This adds a new _Transforms_ submenu to the editor which has exactly one transform in it (for now): strip duplicate spaces.

In the video I am:

* adding some spaces between words
* selecting text that I want to strip duplicate spaces from
* clicking the command from the aforementioned submenu

After running the command the duplicate spaces are stripped: that is, collapsed to a single space.



https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c90fa712-40c6-4235-ae2e-3b911aedf2bc


## Additional information

Tested on: Windows 11 Home.

> Note that I have not updated the CHANGELOG per the PR template guideline 'cos I don't know when this'll be merged, if ever 🤷

summary: Severe slowdown and freeze when using ‘Choose Directory’ in search

description.text:
### Description

**Description**

Trying to limit the global search to a directory slows Zettlr down considerably as soon as the dropdown menu opens.
Clicking on an entry in the dropdown is not possible.
Attempting to choose a sub-directory by typing its name into the dropdown’s input field causes Zettlr to freeze and not recover.
The application must be force-closed and restarted.

⸻

**Steps to Reproduce**
	1.	Click the magnifying glass icon in the upper left corner.
	2.	Enter a search term into the search input field.
	3.	Click on “Choose Directory.”
	4.	The dropdown menu opens.
	5.	Zettlr becomes significantly slower.
	6.	Entries in the dropdown are not clickable.
	7.	Type the name of a sub-directory into the input field.
	8.	Input is possible, but entries remain unclickable.
	9.	To recover, Zettlr must be restarted.

⸻

**Expected Behavior**

The folder dropdown should remain responsive and allow selection via click or text input without any noticeable slowdown or freezing.

⸻

**System Info**
	•	**OS**: macOS Sequoia 15.3.2 (24D81)
	•	**Zettlr** Version: 3.4.3

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/286b116b-2071-4a71-b9ce-02ef7df07b80)

### Reproducing

1.	Click the magnifying glass icon in the upper left corner.
	2.	Enter a search term into the search input field.
	3.	Click on “Choose Directory.”
	4.	The dropdown menu opens.
	5.	Zettlr becomes significantly slower.
	6.	Entries in the dropdown are not clickable.
	7.	Type the name of a sub-directory into the input field.
	8.	Input is possible, but entries remain unclickable.
	9.	To recover, Zettlr must be restarted.


### Zettlr version

3.4.3

### Installation Method

Homebrew

### Installation Method (Other)

Sequoia 15.3.2 (24D81)

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Sequoia 15.3.2 (24D81)

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: fix: Tweak citation selection to be less greedy

description.text:
Fixes #5192

## Description

When clicking on a thing -- such as a citation -- that has a (soft) line-wrap in the middle of the rendered text the current (unwanted) behaviour is that the entirety of all lines that the clicked-on thing spans are selected.

This fixes the behaviour so that *only* the clicked-on thing is selected, even when the clicked-on thing spans multiple lines due to a (soft) line-wrap.

## Before

Notice how text outside the citation is highlighted.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6d77d04e-3601-4854-af6b-7da34a2f51ab

## After

Notice how only the text of the citation is highlighted.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7104a5cb-373b-4008-a795-16c0db57c77f

## Additional information

The fix absolutely 100% belongs to @pradnyaakumbhar: I've just picked up their work from #5470 because it's been languishing in PR limbo. Solely in the interest of getting this over the line -- because it's been mildly annoying me when using Zettlr for my own work -- I've addressed the linting issues and tested it locally. Hopefully this is enought to get it over the line, all hail Pradnya 🙇‍♂️

> I'm not trying to jump Pradnya's train. I'm looking to contribute more to Zettlr and this was a `good first issue` that looked to be stalled.

Tested on: Windows 11 Home

> Note that I have not updated the CHANGELOG per the PR template guideline 'cos I don't know when this'll be merged, if ever 🤷

### Description

This is a sub-issue of #5659.

When inserting quotations from a cited article, users will often need to cut and paste text from PDFs and other documents. Pasted text often needs to be reformatted in the process, which can be time consuming to do by hand. This feature automates one of these reformatting tasks.

Specifically, I propose a CodeMirror command that strips duplicate spaces from a selected region of text. In a not-inconsequential number of PDFs, line breaks in the original text shop up in spaces when copied and pasted into the editor, resulting in text that has extra spaces mid-sentence. So, a conversion that stripped out extra spaces — e.g., converting `technical things  have political qualities` to `technical things have political qualities` — would be quite useful.

This might also come in handy when cleaning text shared by the typewriter generation, who sometimes insert double-spaces between sentences, where only one is now needed.

### Proposed Changes

See #5659.

### Caveats

None.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Nightly

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Operating System Version

Manjaro (Rolling)

### Additional Information

_No response_

### Description

This is a sub-issue of #5659.

When inserting quotations from a cited article, users will often need to cut and paste text from PDFs and other documents. Pasted text often needs to be reformatted in the process, which can be time consuming to do by hand. This feature automates one of these reformatting tasks.

Specifically, I propose a CodeMirror command that converts smart quotes to straight quotes. When selected from a menu (see #5659), this command should convert `“ ‘ ’ ”` to `" ' ' "`.

### Proposed Changes

See #5659.

### Caveats

None.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Nightly

### Your Platform

### Description

`CTRL+P` stopped working.

### Reproducing

I don't know how to reproduce it.

### Zettlr version

3.4.3

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

EndeavourOS

### Additional Information

Till 2 days ago CTRL+P was working fine.

### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [ ] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [ ] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [ ] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

Often when I'm writing or editing a manuscript, I'll debate whether or not to keep a particular paragraph. Or I might cut and paste a section of my notes into the manuscript file. In both cases, I may want to keep the text from appearing in exports unless or until I've decided to delete it. An easy solution is to comment out the lines in question using the HTML comment syntax:

```html
<!--
Yay for comments!
-->
```

The problem is that leaving these big chunks of text can make it hard to read through the remaining manuscript. If it's a long section of notes or a big paragraph, I might have to scroll a bit up and down just to see the rest of the text.

In most code editors, the solution is code folding. You can just click an arrow in the left margin and fold the obtrusive comment out of the way, like so:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9612df2d-c42b-4524-a90d-84e99e67b340)

I'd love to be able to do this in Zettlr. It'd be a fantastic way to be able to tuck notes and tentative sections of the manuscript out of the way to make the remaining text simpler to read in the editor while working.

### Example Workflow

See above.

### Caveats

None.

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

No

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: 1 toggle to switch to raw markdown

description.text:
### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [ ] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

Currently, in order to see the raw markdown one has to do this: Settings > Editor > Markdown Rendering: uncheck all the boxes.

This is a longwinded, primitive way.

### Example Workflow

It would be a workflow improvement if all those check boxes could be unchecked and rechecked with 1 central checkbox, which could also be controlled with a keyboard shortcut.

### Caveats

None.

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

No

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Show more information in the pomodoro timer.

description.text:
### Description

As you're well aware, the pomodoro cycle goes:

- work
- short break
- work
- short break
- work
- short break
- work
- long break
- repeat

In addition to the countdown showing how much time is left until the next element in the cycle, I think it'd be nice to have some indication of one's progress _through_ the cycle.

### Proposed Changes

Maybe the dropdown that shows up when you click on the countdown icon could show something like this:

```
    ▼
🍅 ⌛ 🍅 ⌛ 🍅 ⌛ 🍅 🕐 
```

Or the icons could appear (or change from grayscale to color via a CSS transform) as the user completed them.

It could also be nice, from a feel-good perspective, to show how many total work sessions the user has completed since the session began.

### Caveats

The tomato icon may not be obvious to everyone.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Nightly

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Operating System Version

Manjaro (Rolling)

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Add pause button to pomodoro timer.

description.text:
### Description

I've been using the pomodoro timer more lately and it's super useful. My one issue is that, occasionally, I'll experience a brief interruption — an important incoming message that has to be addressed, someone stopping by my desk, etc. — and there's no way to pause the pomodoro timer when this happens. I can only stop it and start the cycle of pomodoros all over again.

To give an example of where this is sub-optimal, if I've been going for almost two hours, and am interrupted briefly in the middle of my last work session left before my long break, it would be nice to be able to pause the timer for a moment, rather than my only choices being to let the timer run while I talk to a student or hit `Stop` and begin the entire cycle from the top, with four more work sessions to get through before a long break.

Obviously, it's easy enough to just use a non-integrated pomodoro timer that I can pause as needed, but that sort of defeats the purpose of the feature.

### Proposed Changes

See above.

### Caveats

Some people may like the "write or die" nature of having no pause button. But I think it makes sense for most of us.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Nightly

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Operating System Version

Manjaro (Rolling)

### Additional Information

_No response_
